commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor real-behavior proof plus maintainer acceptance of the message-delivery policy, not a narrow code repair for automation.; Cleared: The diff changes only auto-reply TypeScript runtime/tests and does not alter dependencies, workflows, secrets, package metadata, downloads, or external code execution. commands.review_summary:
The PR suppresses failed tool progress in regular verbose immediate and queued auto-reply delivery while preserving full-verbose or forced diagnostics and updating focused tests.

PR surface: Source +34, Tests +64. Total +98 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main forwards failed command, item, and tool-result progress in regular verbose paths. I did not establish a live channel reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor real-behavior proof plus maintainer acceptance of the message-delivery policy, not a narrow code repair for automation.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): hide recovered failed tool progress
target.body:
## Summary

Suppress recovered failed tool progress from regular verbose delivery while keeping full verbose diagnostics visible.

- Hide failed `onCommandOutput`, failed item events, and failed tool-result progress in regular verbose mode.
- Keep failed progress visible in `verbose=full` and for forced tool progress.
- Apply the same policy to queued follow-up runs so delayed progress delivery does not surface recovered helper failures later.

## Why

A transient helper/tool failure can be recovered by a later successful tool call or final assistant response. Showing the failed progress immediately makes the chat look like the turn failed even when the agent recovered and produced a valid answer.

This keeps the actual error classification intact: tool failures still remain in the run state/transcript, and terminal tool-error warnings remain available when the failure is not recovered. The change only gates user-visible progress delivery for ordinary verbose mode.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: In regular verbose delivery, a failed helper command, item event, or tool-result progress event could be shown to the user immediately even when the agent later recovered and produced a successful final reply. This made a recovered turn look failed.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, branch `codex/hide-recovered-failed-progress`, head `076f03256a30d164453efbade434e395e72e6383`, Node/pnpm project test runner.
- Exact focused behavior command run after this patch: `pnpm test -- --run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts -t "regular verbose failed|full verbose failed|queued regular-verbose failed|queued full-verbose" --reporter=verbose`.
- Evidence after fix: focused terminal proof shows the immediate and queued dispatch paths behave correctly after the patch.

```text
✓ dispatchReplyFromConfig > keeps terminal tool-error fallbacks available when regular verbose failed command progress is hidden
✓ dispatchReplyFromConfig > keeps terminal tool-error fallbacks available when regular verbose failed item progress is hidden
✓ dispatchReplyFromConfig > keeps full verbose failed command progress visible
✓ createFollowupRunner progress forwarding > keeps queued regular-verbose failed tool result payloads hidden
✓ createFollowupRunner progress forwarding > keeps queued regular-verbose failed progress hidden across follow-ups
✓ createFollowupRunner progress forwarding > keeps queued full-verbose tool-error fallbacks available after failed progress
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  6 passed | 294 skipped (300)
```

- Observed immediate-dispatch result after fix: regular verbose failed command progress with `status: "failed"` and `exitCode: 1` did not call the channel `onCommandOutput` progress callback, regular verbose failed item progress did not call `onItemEvent`, and the final reply still delivered as `{ text: "done" }`. In `verbose=full`, the same failed command progress did call `onCommandOutput` and the final reply still delivered.
- Observed queued-follow-up result after fix: regular verbose failed tool-result progress was not routed through `routeReply`, regular verbose failed command progress did not call `onCommandOutput` across follow-up runs, and full verbose queued failed progress still called `onCommandOutput` once for diagnostics.
- Full focused test command run after this patch: `pnpm test -- --run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts`.
- Full focused test evidence after fix:

```text
Test Files  2 passed (2)
Tests  300 passed (300)
[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 29.27s
```

- Formatting and whitespace checks after this patch: `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts` passed with `All matched files use the correct format`; `git diff --check` produced no output.
- CI evidence after fix: GitHub `Real behavior proof` passed for this head in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28275098862.
- What was not tested: A native Telegram Desktop before/after GIF was attempted by Mantis in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/28274357137, but that run did not produce the needed recovered failed tool-progress sequence followed by a successful reply, so it uploaded only a failure manifest and no visual artifact. A full repository `tsc` run was also attempted with the default heap and with `NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=8192`; both aborted with Node heap OOM before reporting TypeScript diagnostics.

## Tests

- `pnpm test -- --run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts -t "regular verbose failed|full verbose failed|queued regular-verbose failed|queued full-verbose" --reporter=verbose`
- `pnpm test -- --run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.ts src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
target.author: litang9
